html:
[411]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 执挚即贽愿从学 - Formal expression meaning 'holding a gift and wishing to follow in learning.'
  • 取书观，有悟 - Taking a book to read, having an insight.
  • 才把笔，即能画字 - Just picking up a brush, immediately being able to write characters.
  • 其字每类予，见者辄谓予假为之 - His characters often resemble mine, and those who see them always say I have written them for him.
  • 心痛遽罢 - Suddenly stopping due to heart pain.
  • 学书不学卫夫人，别有簪花体格新 - Learning to write without following Wei Fu, there is a new style of flower-like writing.
  • 夫人才把笔，便作逸少字 - The lady just picks up the brush, and immediately writes like the style of Yi Shao.
  • 檀板能歌绝妙词，银钩学写相思字 - The wooden board can sing the most wonderful words, the silver hook can write words of longing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 曼殊 - A proper name, likely a person's name.
  • 任黄门 - Refers to an official position in the Yellow Gate, a part of the imperial court.
  • 检讨 - A censor or official responsible for reviewing and correcting documents.
  • 方编修 - Refers to an official responsible for compiling and revising texts.
  • 吴文学 - Refers to an official position in the literary bureau of Wu.
  • 施侍读 - Refers to an official position in the imperial library.
  • 朱供奉 - Refers to an official position in the imperial music bureau.
  • 卫夫人 - A famous calligrapher from the Jin dynasty.
  • 逸少 - Another name for Wang Xizhi, a famous calligrapher from the Jin dynasty.
  • 檀板 - A wooden board used in music.
  • 银钩 - A silver hook, a metaphor for writing.

Proper Nouns

  • 曼殊 - A proper name.
  • 任黄门辰旦 - An official position and name.
  • 检讨 - A censor or official position.
  • 方编修 - An official position.
  • 吴文学陈琰 - An official position and name.
  • 施侍读闰章 - An official position and name.
  • 朱供奉 - An official position.
  • 卫夫人 - A famous calligrapher.
  • 逸少 - Another name for Wang Xizhi, a famous calligrapher.

[412]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 于是杂弄简编 - Classical phrase meaning 'thus, amidst handling of books and scrolls,' indicating a scholarly or literary activity.
  • 间亲文吏 - Means 'occasionally engaging with officials,' suggesting a mix of scholarly and administrative duties.
  • 画眉楼畔，即是书林 - Metaphorical expression where '画眉楼' (Huamei Tower) and '书林' (forest of books) evoke a place of literary and cultural refinement.
  • 傅粉房中，便成家垫 - Metaphorical expression where '傅粉房' (room for applying makeup) and '家垫' (home base) suggest a domestic setting transformed by literary pursuits.
  • 学新声于弦上，询难字于枕间 - Metaphorical expressions where '学新声于弦上' (learning new tunes on the strings) and '询难字于枕间' (inquiring about difficult characters in the pillow) evoke a blend of musical and literary activities.
  • 硬黄纸滑，窃书夫子之衔 - Metaphorical expression where '硬黄纸滑' (smooth hard yellow paper) and '窃书夫子之衔' (stealing the title of a scholar) suggest a playful or mischievous act.
  • 缥碧钗轻，戏作门生之贽 - Metaphorical expression where '缥碧钗轻' (light blue hairpin) and '戏作门生之贽' (playfully creating a gift for a disciple) evoke a lighthearted and affectionate gesture.
  • 瞥见仙姝漫七年 - Metaphorical expression where '瞥见仙姝' (glimpsing a celestial beauty) and '漫七年' (seven years) suggest a long-lasting and enchanting encounter.
  • 每闻素腕写鸾笺 - Metaphorical expression where '素腕' (white wrist) and '写鸾笺' (writing on phoenix paper) evoke a graceful and elegant writing process.
  • 学得簪花字体新 - Metaphorical expression where '学得簪花字体新' (learning a new flower-like script) suggests the acquisition of a refined and artistic writing style.
  • 蛮笺十幅簇芳茵 - Metaphorical expression where '蛮笺' (foreign paper) and '簇芳茵' (clustered with fragrant grass) suggest a luxurious and ornate writing material.
  • 修成外传多情思 - Metaphorical expression where '修成外传' (completing an external record) and '多情思' (full of emotions) suggest a work filled with emotional depth.
  • 为有灯前拥髻人 - Metaphorical expression where '灯前拥髻人' (person holding a hairpin by the lamp) suggests a close and intimate companion.
  • 曼陀花一朵 - Metaphorical expression where '曼陀花' (Mandala flower) suggests a rare and beautiful bloom.
  • 看向日边枯 - Metaphorical expression where '看向日边枯' (looking towards the sun's edge) suggests a sense of decline or fading beauty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陈序云 - Refers to Chen Xu, a historical figure known for his literary works.
  • 张检讨鸿烈 - Refers to Zhang Jiantao Honglie, a historical figure known for his poetry.
  • 潘检讨耒 - Refers to Pan Jiantao Lei, a historical figure known for his poetry.
  • 予 - Refers to the author of the text, who is likely a scholar or poet.
  • 曼殊病 - A term that may refer to a specific illness or condition, possibly named after a person or place.
  • 真定夫子 - Refers to a respected teacher or scholar from Zhending, a historical place.
  • 祝家园 - Refers to a garden or estate associated with the family name Zhu.
  • 桂枝香 - A type of poetry or song, often associated with the fragrance of osmanthus flowers.
  • 赏心乐事 - A phrase meaning 'pleasant and enjoyable activities,' often used in classical Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 陈序云 - Chen Xu, a historical figure.
  • 张检讨鸿烈 - Zhang Jiantao Honglie, a historical figure.
  • 潘检讨耒 - Pan Jiantao Lei, a historical figure.
  • 真定夫子 - A respected teacher or scholar from Zhending.
  • 祝家园 - A garden or estate associated with the family name Zhu.
  • 桂枝香 - A type of poetry or song.
  • 赏心乐事 - A phrase meaning 'pleasant and enjoyable activities.'

[413]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 从来绣阁惜娉婷 - 从 the beginning or always; 绣阁 a woman's boudoir; 惜娉婷 cherish the beautiful and graceful.
  • 红牙欲按声转停 - 红牙 a red ivory clapper used in music; 欲按 about to play; 声转停 the sound pauses.
  • 闻君雅擅周郎顾 - 闻君 I hear you; 雅擅 excel at; 周郎顾 reference to Zhou Yu, a famous general and musician from the Three Kingdoms period, known for his ability to recognize talent.
  • 妾若歌时君细听 - 妾若歌时 when I sing; 君细听 please listen carefully.
  • 学书便仿簪花格 - 学书 to learn calligraphy; 便仿 to imitate; 簪花格 a style of calligraphy that resembles flowers on a hairpin.
  • 偷曲初成按拍时 - 偷曲 to secretly learn a tune; 初成 just completed; 按拍时 when the rhythm is being set.
  • 拙宦中年何草草 - 拙宦 a mediocre official career; 中年 middle age; 何草草 how haphazardly.
  • 但看曼殊愁顿扫 - 但看 just look; 曼殊 a Buddhist name; 愁顿扫 suddenly dispel sorrow.
  • 酒阑一唱《祝家词》 - 酒阑 when the wine is finished; 一唱 to sing once; 《祝家词》 a specific song or poem.
  • 温柔乡里真堪老 - 温柔乡 a place of comfort and ease; 真堪老 truly suitable for growing old.
  • 冰弦檀板两怡然 - 冰弦 a cold string (referring to a musical instrument); 檀板 a wooden clapper; 两怡然 both are content.
  • 花底征歌月底眠 - 花底征歌 singing under the flowers; 月底眠 sleeping under the moon.
  • 百绾云{髟火}巧样成 - 百绾 many twists and turns; 云{髟火} a complex character referring to a hairstyle; 巧样成 skillfully formed.
  • 淡黄裙子称身轻 - 淡黄 light yellow; 裙子 skirt; 称身轻 fits the body perfectly.
  • 清歌按板偏能会 - 清歌 clear singing; 按板 setting the rhythm; 偏能会 especially able to understand.
  • 不数红红记豆名 - 不数 not counted among; 红红 a nickname; 记豆名 remembering the names of beans (a metaphor for trivial matters).
  • 新翻《子夜》与《前溪》 - 新翻 newly composed; 《子夜》 and 《前溪》 specific songs.
  • 顾曲周郎总不迷 - 顾曲 to appreciate music; 周郎 reference to Zhou Yu; 总不迷 never gets lost.
  • 一唱黄鸡娇欲绝 - 一唱 to sing once; 黄鸡 a bird; 娇欲绝 extremely charming.
  • 萧声同彻凤楼西 - 萧声 sound of the flute; 同彻 echoes together; 凤楼西 west of the phoenix pavilion.
  • 歌残《金缕》不胜悲 - 歌残 the song ends; 《金缕》 a specific song; 不胜悲 cannot bear the sadness.
  • 记得南园卧病时 - 记得 remember; 南园 south garden; 卧病时 when ill.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 冯太傅 - Feng Taifu, a historical figure.
  • 周郎 - Zhou Yu, a famous general and musician from the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 曼殊 - A Buddhist name, often associated with compassion.
  • 《祝家词》 - A specific song or poem.
  • 温柔乡 - A metaphor for a place of comfort and ease.
  • 冰弦檀板 - Musical instruments.
  • 花底征歌月底眠 - Metaphorical expressions related to singing and sleeping.
  • 田编修 - Tian Bianxiu, a historical figure.
  • 淡黄裙子 - A light yellow skirt.
  • 清歌按板 - Clear singing and setting the rhythm.
  • 红红 - A nickname.
  • 胡文学 - Hu Wenxue, a historical figure.
  • 《子夜》与《前溪》 - Specific songs.
  • 王光禄三杰 - Wang Guangluo Sanjie, a historical figure.
  • 《金缕》 - A specific song.

Proper Nouns

  • 冯太傅 - Feng Taifu
  • 周郎 - Zhou Yu
  • 曼殊 - A Buddhist name
  • 《祝家词》 - A specific song or poem
  • 温柔乡 - A metaphorical place
  • 冰弦檀板 - Musical instruments
  • 花底征歌月底眠 - Metaphorical expressions
  • 田编修 - Tian Bianxiu
  • 淡黄裙子 - A light yellow skirt
  • 清歌按板 - Clear singing and setting the rhythm
  • 红红 - A nickname
  • 胡文学 - Hu Wenxue
  • 《子夜》与《前溪》 - Specific songs
  • 王光禄三杰 - Wang Guangluo Sanjie
  • 《金缕》 - A specific song

[414]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夜起與郎花下坐 - Literally means 'at night, rising to sit under the flowers with the young man.' Sets a romantic and poetic scene.
  • 含颦一唱《祝家词》 - '含颦' means to frown or pout slightly, adding an emotional nuance to the act of singing. '《祝家词》' refers to a specific song or poem.
  • 曼殊自为诗云 - '曼殊' likely refers to a person named Man Shu, who composed a poem. The phrase '自为诗云' means 'composed a poem saying.'
  • 酒阑携锦瑟，请唱《祝家园》 - '酒阑' means the end of a drinking party, '携锦瑟' means to carry a lute, and '请唱《祝家园》' means to request the singing of 'The Garden of the Zhu Family.'
  • 苦无弹者 - Means 'there was no one to play it,' indicating a problem with finding someone to play the lute.
  • 谛视其拢撚{百刂}拨 - '谛视' means to observe closely, '拢撚{百刂}拨' refers to the techniques of playing the lute, including plucking and strumming.
  • 遂能弹 - Means 'and thus could play,' indicating that after observing, the person learned to play.
  • 朱供奉《洞庭秋色》词云 - '朱供奉' refers to a person named Zhu, who served in a high office. '《洞庭秋色》' is a specific poem or song about the autumn scenery of Dongting Lake.
  • 想暗通心曲，朱丝弦里 - '想暗通心曲' means to think that the music conveys hidden emotions, '朱丝弦里' means in the red silk strings of the lute.
  • 尽携书卷，玉镜台前 - '尽携书卷' means to carry all books, '玉镜台前' means in front of a jade mirror stand, often used to symbolize a place of study or writing.
  • 尤检讨侗《新样四时花》曲云 - '尤检讨侗' refers to a person named You, who served as an advisor. '《新样四时花》' is a specific song or poem about the four seasons' flowers, possibly a new composition.
  • 罗敷赵瑟侬家占，《子夜》吴歌近日谙 - '罗敷赵瑟侬家占' means 'the lady Luo Fu claims the Zhaosu lute,' '《子夜》吴歌近日谙' means 'I recently learned the Wu songs of the night.'
  • 袁编修佑诗云 - '袁编修佑' refers to a person named Yuan, who served as an editor-in-chief. '诗云' means 'the poem says.'
  • 郎自艳吴曲，侬自缓秦筝 - '郎自艳吴曲' means 'the young man admires the Wu tunes,' '侬自缓秦筝' means 'I slowly play the Qin zither.'
  • 双栖梁上燕，解语弄春声 - '双栖梁上燕' means 'two swallows nesting on the beam,' '解语弄春声' means 'understanding words and playing spring sounds.'
  • 冯检讨勖诗云 - '冯检讨勖' refers to a person named Feng, who served as an advisor. '诗云' means 'the poem says.'
  • 细抛红豆谱相思，肠断金槽一缕丝 - '细抛红豆谱相思' means 'carefully throwing red beans to compose a love song,' '肠断金槽一缕丝' means 'heartbroken by the golden slot and a thread of silk.'
  • 谁道梁尘惊散后，酒阑犹唱《祝家词》 - '谁道梁尘惊散后' means 'who says after the dust in the hall has been startled and scattered,' '酒阑犹唱《祝家词》' means 'at the end of the drinking party, still singing 'The Song of the Zhu Family.''
  • 吴别驾融诗云 - '吴别驾融' refers to a person named Wu, who served as a deputy governor. '诗云' means 'the poem says.'
  • 渌水春来艳，金槽夜自弹 - '渌水春来艳' means 'the green water comes in spring and is beautiful,' '金槽夜自弹' means 'the golden slot plays itself at night.'
  • 市楼盲女在，莫作段师看 - '市楼盲女在' means 'the blind girl in the market building is there,' '莫作段师看' means 'do not look at her as a teacher.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《祝家词》 - A specific song or poem, likely from the Zhu family, known for their literary contributions.
  • 《洞庭秋色》 - A poem or song about the autumn scenery of Dongting Lake, a famous scenic area in China.
  • 《新样四时花》 - A song or poem about the four seasons' flowers, possibly a new composition.
  • 《子夜》吴歌 - Refers to the 'Midnight Wu Songs,' a type of folk song from the Wu region.
  • 《祝家园》 - A song or poem about the garden of the Zhu family.

[415]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 顧得奇病 - Conveniently or happily acquired a strange illness; suggests a somewhat ironic or resigned tone.
  • 初书刺心痛 - Initially recorded piercing heart pain; indicates the first symptom reported.
  • 谓脘寒也 - Called stomach cold; initial diagnosis.
  • 既谓伤肝 - Later called liver injury; subsequent diagnosis.
  • 输东风 - Transferred east wind; metaphorical expression related to the liver's association with the east in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 木扬 - Wood rises; could be a metaphor for the liver's function or a reference to the liver's element in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 春作而秋止 - Spring onset and autumn cessation; describes the seasonal pattern of the illness.
  • 又既谓中懣 - Later called central depression; another diagnosis.
  • 有瘕癖 - Has a mass and habit; suggests a persistent condition.
  • 在胃傍 - Near the stomach; specifies the location of the condition.
  • 气积不行 - Qi accumulation not moving; describes the nature of the condition.
  • 历数载 - For several years; indicates the duration of the illness.
  • 审候 - Carefully observe; refers to the process of diagnosis.
  • 终不得其要领 - Ultimately could not grasp the essentials; indicates the difficulty in finding a cure.
  • 每疾作 - Whenever the disease occurs; describes the recurrence of symptoms.
  • 遍体若煿 - Entire body feels as if roasted; describes the sensation experienced during an episode.
  • 使婢按摩之 - Have the maid massage it; describes a treatment method.
  • 不足，以帔作兜 - If not enough, use a shawl as a hammock; describes an additional treatment method.
  • 负之行 - Carry it while walking; describes a further treatment method.
  • 又不足，缒筐而坐之 - If still not enough, hang a basket and sit in it; describes another treatment method.
  • 东西推挽 - Push and pull east and west; describes the movement of the basket.
  • 若鞦韆然 - Like a swing; compares the movement to a swing.
  • 任黄门传云 - According to the official historian's records; introduces a quote from historical records.
  • 然有奇疾 - Indeed has a strange illness; confirms the nature of the illness.
  • 疾剧，则必约綵为兜 - When the disease is severe, they must tie colorful fabric into a hammock; describes a treatment method.
  • 有若花篮 - Looks like a flower basket; describes the appearance of the hammock.
  • 坐其中 - Sit inside it; describes the position during treatment.
  • 悬诸空际 - Hang it in mid-air; describes the method of hanging the hammock.
  • 左旋右转 - Rotate left and right; describes the movement of the hammock.
  • 乃少可 - Then slightly better; indicates the effect of the treatment.
  • 特终不可治 - But ultimately cannot be cured; indicates the incurability of the illness.
  • 尝遍搜方术 - Have tried all medical methods; indicates the extensive efforts to find a cure.
  • 不治 - Not cured; confirms the failure of the treatments.
  • 遂立愿舍身作佛弟子 - Therefore vowed to give up their body and become a Buddhist disciple; describes the decision to seek spiritual salvation.
  • 不治，乃召绘者图之 - Not cured, then summoned a painter to depict it; describes the action of having the illness depicted.
  • 名曰《留视图》云 - Named it *The Picture of Lingering Gaze*; gives the title of the painting.
  • 已而竟不可治 - Later, it still could not be cured; reiterates the incurability of the illness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 任黄门传 - Refers to the official historian's records, likely from the *Book of Han* or similar historical texts.
  • 佛弟子 - Refers to a Buddhist disciple, indicating a religious conversion in response to the illness.
  • 《留视图》 - The title of the painting, which suggests a visual representation of the illness and its treatment.

Proper Nouns

  • 任黄门传 - The official historian's records, likely from the *Book of Han*.
  • 佛弟子 - A Buddhist disciple.
  • 《留视图》 - The title of the painting.

[416]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 病倚篮舆挹翠霞 - Sickly leaning on a sedan chair to savor the green clouds, describing a woman's delicate and ethereal beauty.
  • 后庭编径曲栏斜 - Describes a winding path with curved railings in the back courtyard, evoking a sense of elegance and seclusion.
  • 彩兜行遍虽无迹 - Wearing colorful headscarves, she has walked everywhere without leaving a trace, suggesting a light and almost ghostly presence.
  • 犹长金莲处处花 - Still growing lotus flowers everywhere, a metaphor for her beauty spreading like blooming flowers.
  • 本吾家物 - Originally belong to my family, indicating a sense of belonging or destiny.
  • 挤眼 - Wink, a subtle gesture often associated with communication or invitation.
  • 随我行 - Follow me, a command or request to accompany someone.
  • 家去吧！不去，奶奶么喝 - Go home! If you don't, the old lady will scold you, a colloquial and somewhat threatening phrase.
  • 流涕送庙间 - Crying while sending her to the temple, indicating a poignant farewell.
  • 淡红香白好容颜 - A good face with light red and fragrant white, describing a beautiful and fragrant appearance.
  • 宝髻堆云作百鬟 - A precious hair bun piled up like clouds into a hundred buns, a detailed description of elaborate hair styling.
  • 唤作佛花元自误 - Calling her a Buddha flower was originally a mistake, suggesting a misinterpretation or misunderstanding.
  • 如今争肯住人间 - Now, who would want to stay in the human world, expressing a desire to leave the mortal realm.
  • 阿钱生小态婵娟 - Little Ah Qian has a graceful and charming appearance, describing a young girl's beauty.
  • 多病皈依绣佛前 - Sickly, she has taken refuge before the embroidered Buddha, indicating a turn to religious devotion due to illness.
  • 不信曼陀花一朵 - Doubting the existence of a single lotus flower, suggesting skepticism or disbelief.
  • 忍教憔悴夕阳天 - How can one bear to see her withered under the setting sun, expressing pity and sorrow.
  • 妖梦频随阿母回 - Frequent demonic dreams following her back to her mother, indicating recurring nightmares.
  • 香檀分影礼莲台 - Dividing the shadow of fragrant sandalwood to worship the lotus platform, a ritualistic and spiritual act.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 篮舆 - A sedan chair, commonly used by women in ancient China for transportation.
  • 翠霞 - Green clouds, often used in poetry to describe the sky or a beautiful woman's appearance.
  • 后庭 - The back courtyard, a private and secluded area in traditional Chinese homes.
  • 编径曲栏 - Winding paths with curved railings, typical of classical Chinese gardens.
  • 彩兜 - Colorful headscarves, a common accessory for women in ancient China.
  • 金莲 - A metaphor for a woman's small feet, symbolizing beauty and delicacy.
  • 佛花 - Buddha flower, a term that can refer to a lotus flower or a metaphorical representation of purity and enlightenment.
  • 绣佛 - Embroidered Buddha, a religious icon or image of the Buddha.
  • 曼陀花 - A type of lotus flower, often associated with purity and spiritual significance.
  • 夕阳天 - Setting sun, a symbol of the end of the day and sometimes used to evoke a sense of melancholy.
  • 妖梦 - Demonic dreams, often associated with supernatural or eerie experiences.
  • 香檀 - Fragrant sandalwood, a material often used in religious rituals and offerings.
  • 莲台 - Lotus platform, a symbol of purity and enlightenment in Buddhism.

Proper Nouns

  • 陆文学宏定 - Lu Wenzue Hongding, the author of the poem.
  • 赵编修执信 - Zhao Bianxiu Zhi Xin, another poet mentioned in the text.
  • 吴文学陈琰 - Wu Wenxue Chen Yan, another poet mentioned in the text.

[417]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 百鬟巧髻 - Describes a hairstyle with many intricate braids, often associated with beauty and elegance in classical Chinese literature.
  • 親留視 - Literally means 'to look at carefully,' but in this context, it likely refers to the act of closely examining or cherishing the image of the person.
  • 畫里真真 - A playful reference to the idea of a painting coming to life, possibly alluding to the famous story of 'Hua Shan Lian' (花山恋), where a painting of a woman comes to life.
  • 雕香分送淚模糊 - Describes the act of sending fragrant incense, with the tears being blurred, suggesting a moment of emotional intensity or confusion.
  • 六尺生绡 - Refers to a six-foot-long piece of silk or gauze, often used for painting or writing.
  • 白衣龛外立 - Describes a person standing outside a white-robed shrine or altar, possibly symbolizing purity or a spiritual presence.
  • 前身應是小龙姑 - Suggests that the person being described was a previous incarnation of a young dragon maiden, a common theme in Chinese mythology.
  • 荷花开作面，菊叶剪为衣 - Describes a person whose face resembles a lotus flower and whose clothes are made from chrysanthemum leaves, symbolizing beauty and fragility.

  • 往复不休 - 往复：来回；不休：不停止。描述动作的反复不停。
  • 掩泪归舟 - 掩泪：擦去眼泪；归舟：返回船只。描述擦去眼泪后返回船只。
  • 伫立已久 - 伫立：站立等待；已久：已经很久。描述长时间站立等待。
  • 劝慰无从 - 劝慰：安慰；无从：没有办法。描述无法安慰的情况。
  • 徘徊丘垄间 - 徘徊：徘徊；丘垄：坟墓。描述在坟墓间徘徊。
  • 自经于台畔 - 自经：自缢；台畔：平台旁边。描述在平台旁自缢。
  • 大难大难 - 大难：大灾难；大难：大灾难。描述深重的苦难或哀痛。
  • 合葬之墓 - 合葬：合葬；之墓：坟墓。描述合葬的坟墓。
  • 倾城士人閧传感叹 - 倾城：全城；士人：读书人；閧传感叹：感动叹息。描述全城读书人感动叹息。
  • 络绎喧豗 - 络绎：连续不断；喧豗：喧闹。描述连续不断的喧闹人群。
  • 云蒸雨集 - 云蒸：云气上升；雨集：雨水聚集。描述云气上升雨水聚集，常比喻大量人群聚集。
  • 哀声动地 - 哀声：哀痛的声音；动地：震动地面。描述哀痛的声音震动地面。
  • 殆莫知其由也 - 殆：大概；莫知：没有人知道；其由：原因。描述大概没有人知道原因。
  • 植碑题其上 - 植碑：立碑；题其上：题写在上面。描述立碑并在上面题写。
  • 命崔恩居之 - 命：命令；崔恩：人名；居之：居住在那里。描述命令崔恩居住在那里。
  • 供春秋奠扫之役 - 供：提供；春秋：春秋季；奠扫：祭扫；之役：职责。描述提供春秋季祭扫的职责。
  • 通体衫襦 - 通体：全身；衫襦：衣服。描述全身穿着衣服。
  • 细缀严密无少隙 - 细缀：精细缝制；严密：紧密；无少隙：没有缝隙。描述精细缝制紧密无隙的衣服。
  • 知其矢死已久 - 知：知道；其：她；矢死：决心赴死；已久：已经很久。描述知道她已经很久决心赴死。
  • 具棺衾 - 具：准备；棺：棺材；衾：寿衣。描述准备棺材和寿衣。
  • 易服敛之 - 易服：更换衣服；敛之：包裹她。描述更换衣服并包裹她。

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 呜呼 - An interjection expressing sorrow or lamentation, often used at the beginning of a lament or eulogy.
  • 庶乎 - Means 'perhaps' or 'hopefully,' expressing a wish or expectation.
  • 灵、莹之事 - Refers to the story of Ling and Ying, likely a reference to a famous literary work or legend.
  • 六如之事 - Refers to the story of Liu Ru, possibly a reference to a historical or literary figure.
  • 躬诣墓所拜奠 - Means 'personally visit the tomb to pay respects,' indicating a solemn act of remembrance.
  • 辗转不寐 - Means 'restlessly unable to sleep,' describing a state of agitation or deep thought.
  • 启窗纵目 - Means 'open the window and look out,' suggesting a moment of reflection or observation.
  • 消受香光 - Means 'to enjoy the fragrance and light,' metaphorically referring to the afterlife or spiritual comfort.
  • 差可不负矣 - Means 'can be somewhat justified' or 'can be somewhat satisfactory,' expressing a sense of partial fulfillment.
  • 欷歔泣下 - Means 'to sob and weep,' describing a moment of intense emotion.
  • 花满山中高士卧，月明林下美人来 - A poetic line that can be interpreted as 'flowers fill the mountains where a noble recluse lies, and under the bright moon, a beautiful woman comes to the woods.' This line is likely from a classical poem.
  • 君以我为真死耶？ - Means 'Do you think I am truly dead?' expressing a question about the nature of death.
  • 死者形，不死者性 - Means 'The body dies, but the spirit does not,' a philosophical statement about the immortality of the soul.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吾既为一世才子 - Self-referential statement meaning 'I, having been a talented scholar of my time.'
  • 死后岂若他人泯没耶 - Interrogative phrase meaning 'After death, how could I be like others who are forgotten?'
  • 今乘此花满山中 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Now, taking advantage of this time when flowers cover the mountains.'
  • 高士偃卧 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the recluses are lying down.'
  • 特来造访耳 - Concluding phrase meaning 'I have come specifically to visit.'
  • 此非『月明林下美人来』乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is this not the 'beauty comes under the moonlight in the forest'?'
  • 六如回顾 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Six-As looked back.'
  • 姗姗来前 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'came gracefully forward.'
  • 崔莹也 - Proper name, meaning 'Cui Ying.'
  • 两人携手整襟 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the two of them held hands and straightened their clothes.'
  • 向六如拜谢合葬之德 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'they bowed to Six-As in gratitude for his kindness in burying them together.'
  • 六如方扶掖之 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Six-As was just supporting her.'
  • 忽又闻有人大呼曰 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'suddenly, someone shouted loudly.'
  • 我高季迪梅花诗 - Proper name and title, meaning 'My poem 'Plum Blossoms' by Gao Jidi.'
  • 乃千古绝唱 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'is a timeless masterpiece.'
  • 何物张灵 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'what sort of Zhang Ling.'
  • 妄称才子 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'unjustly claims to be a talented scholar.'
  • 改雪为花 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'changed snow to flowers.'
  • 定须饱我老拳 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'must surely be beaten by my fist.'
  • 六如转瞬之间 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'in the blink of an eye.'
  • 灵、莹俱失所在 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Zhang Ling and Cui Ying disappeared.'
  • 直前呼曰 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'came forward and shouted.'
  • 当捶此改诗之贼才子 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'should beat this thief who altered the poem.'
  • 捽六如欲殴之 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'grabbed Six-As and wanted to beat him.'
  • 六如惊寐 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Six-As woke up in shock.'
  • 则半窗明月，阒其无人 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'then half the window was lit by moonlight, and the room was empty.'
  • 六如怃然 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Six-As was filled with a sense of loss.'
  • 始信真才子与真佳人，盖死而不死也 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'began to believe that true talented scholars and true beauties are immortal.'
  • 因匡坐梅窗下 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'therefore sat upright under the plum window.'
  • 作《张灵、崔莹合传》 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'wrote the 'Combined Biography of Zhang Ling and Cui Ying.'
  • 以纪其事 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'to record the event.'
  • 然今日《六如集》中 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'however, in today's 'Six-As Collection.'
  • 固未尝见此传也 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'it has never been seen before.'
  • 余又安得而不亟补之哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'how could I not urgently add it?'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 畸史氏曰 - Formal opening used by the author to introduce their commentary or opinion.
  • 嗟乎 - Interjection expressing sorrow or lamentation.
  • 稗官家言 - Refers to unofficial or popular stories, often used to describe tales that may not be entirely reliable.
  • 真赝参半 - Means that the stories are a mix of true and false.
  • 章章 - Means 'clearly' or 'distinctly,' emphasizing the clarity of the reference.
  • 但素琼之事，无从考证 - Indicates that there is no way to verify the truth of the story about Suqiong.
  • 有其事何必无其人 - Rhetorical question suggesting that if something is true, it is reasonable to assume that the person involved also exists.
  • 作者有为而发 - Suggests that the author had a specific purpose or intention in writing the story.
  • 目空千古 - Means 'to look down upon all of history,' indicating a high level of self-confidence or arrogance.
  • 尚论才子佳人 - Refers to the evaluation or discussion of talented men and beautiful women.
  • 耑以太白与莺莺当之 - Means 'to specifically match Li Bai and Yingying as examples.'
  • 厥后果遇素琼 - Indicates that the outcome was meeting Suqiong.
  • 思崔得崔 - Play on words, suggesting that thinking of Cui led to meeting Cui, implying a fulfillment of destiny.
  • 适符其谶 - Means 'just as the prophecy predicted.'
  • 张以情死，崔以情殉 - Describes Zhang's death due to love and Cui's sacrifice due to love.
  • 初非有一词半缕之成约 - Means 'they did not have any prearranged agreement or promise.'
  • 慷慨从容，等泰山于鸿毛 - Describes a bold and composed attitude, treating death as light as a feather compared to Mount Tai.
  • 徒以才色相怜之故 - Means 'simply because they were attracted to each other's talent and beauty.'
  • 凛凛生气，日月争光 - Describes a vivid and intense life force, competing with the brightness of the sun and moon.
  • 远出琴心犊鼻之上 - Means 'far surpassing the subtlety of the lute and the simplicity of the oxhide.'
  • 追恨于梦晋之蚤死 - Expresses regret for the early death of Mengjin.
  • 素琼遣归之日，正崔、张好合之年 - Indicates that the day Suqiong was sent back home was the same year that Cui and Zhang were united.
  • 白头唱和，兰玉盈阶 - Describes a happy and prosperous future, with white hair and harmonious singing, and children filling the steps.
  • 庸庸蚩蚩者之厚福 - Refers to the good fortune of ordinary people.
  • 何有于才子佳人哉 - Rhetorical question suggesting that such good fortune is not something that talented men and beautiful women would have.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蓮游於酒人 - Literal meaning 'Lotus wanders among wine drinkers,' likely referring to a person named Lotus who frequents places where wine is served, possibly indicating a lifestyle or profession.
  • 所致金钱随手尽 - Means 'the money he receives is spent immediately,' suggesting a tendency to spend money freely or quickly.
  • 尤喜为窭儒画 - 'Especially likes to paint for poor scholars,' indicating a preference or habit of creating artwork for those of modest means.
  • 窭儒借莲画给空 - 'Poor scholars borrow Lotus's paintings to fill their homes,' implying that his works are valued and sought after by less affluent individuals.
  • 豪家索之，千缗勿得也 - 'The wealthy families demand them, but even a thousand strings of cash cannot obtain them,' highlighting the value and exclusivity of his art.
  • 尝为诸生 - 'Once was a student,' indicating a past role or status.
  • 督学使索之，亦勿得 - 'The supervisor of education demands them, but they too cannot get them,' further emphasizing the high demand for his work.
  • 顾生平好妇人 - 'But throughout his life, he favored women,' suggesting a personal preference or interest.
  • 非妇人在坐不饮 - 'He would not drink unless women were present,' indicating a social habit or requirement.
  • 夕寝非妇人不得寐 - 'He could not sleep without a woman by his side at night,' again emphasizing his preference for the company of women.
  • 有携妇人乞画，辄应云 - 'When someone brought a woman to ask for a painting, he would agree,' showing a willingness to create art under certain conditions.
  • 崇祯末，愍皇帝命供奉，不拜，寻以兵罢 - 'At the end of the reign of Emperor Chongzhen, the Emperor of Mercy ordered him to serve, but he refused, and soon after, he was dismissed due to military affairs,' detailing a significant event in his life.
  • 监国中，待诏 - 'During the regency, he was appointed as a court painter,' indicating a period of service to the state.
  • 王师下浙东，大将军抚军固山，从围城中搜得莲，大喜，急令画，不画；刃迫之，不画；以酒与妇人诱之画 - 'When the royal army marched into eastern Zhejiang, the great general, the pacifier of the army, and the governor of Guishan, searched for Lotus within the besieged city, greatly pleased, urgently ordered him to paint, but he refused; they threatened him with swords, but he still refused; they offered wine and women to entice him to paint,' describing a dramatic encounter with the military.
  • 久之，请汇所为画署名，且有粉本 - 'After some time, he asked to compile all his paintings, sign them, and also have a model for the paintings,' indicating a request for recognition and documentation of his work.
  • 渲染已，大饮，夜抱画寝 - 'After finishing the painting, he had a big drink, and at night, he held the painting while sleeping,' suggesting a moment of celebration and personal attachment to his work.
  • 及伺之，遁矣！ - 'When they watched him, he had already fled!' indicating his escape from the situation.
  • 朝鲜、兀良哈、日本、撤马儿罕、乌思藏购莲画，重其直 - 'Korea, Wulanh, Japan, Sarmarhan, and U-Tsang purchased Lotus's paintings, valuing them highly,' showing the international demand for his art.
  • 海内传模为生者数千家 - 'Within the country, thousands of families made a living by copying his paintings,' indicating the widespread influence and economic impact of his work.
  • 甬东袁鹍贫，为洋船典簿记，藏莲画两幅截竹中，将归，贻日本主 - 'In Yongdong, Yuan Kun, who was poor, worked as a clerk on a foreign ship, hiding two of Lotus's paintings in bamboo sections, intending to return them to the Japanese lord,' detailing a specific incident involving the transportation of his art.
  • 主大喜，重予宴，酬以囊珠，亦传模笔也 - 'The lord was greatly pleased, hosting a grand banquet, rewarding him with a bag of pearls, and it was also a copy of the brushwork,' indicating the high value placed on his art and the reward given for it.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 模周長史畫 - Copying the painting of Zhou, the Chief Secretary; refers to the act of imitating a famous painting by Zhou, a high-ranking official.
  • 至再三，猶不欲已 - Up to three times, still not wanting to stop; indicates a strong desire to continue copying the painting.
  • 此畫已過周，而猶嗛嗛 - This painting has surpassed Zhou, yet still feels unsatisfied; the term '嗛嗛' suggests a sense of modesty or dissatisfaction despite success.
  • 吾畫易見好 - My painting is easily liked; implies that the painting is simple and lacks depth.
  • 長史本至能，而若無能 - The Chief Secretary is originally very skilled, yet appears as if he has no skill; suggests that Zhou's skill is so natural and effortless that it seems like he has no skill at all.
  • 氣韻兼力 - Combining spirit and power; describes a painting that is both dynamic and expressive.
  • 沨沨容容 - Flowing and graceful; describes a painting that is fluid and elegant.
  • 勾绰捉勒 - Drawing and capturing; refers to the technique of outlining and detailing in a painting.
  • 隨境堑错 - Following the environment and creating intricate patterns; describes a painting that adapts to its surroundings and creates complex designs.
  • 驅遣于法度之中 - Driving within the bounds of rules; suggests that the painting adheres to established artistic principles.
  • 钉前燕后 - Pinning before and after; metaphorically means to establish a strong foundation and a lasting legacy.
  • 陵轹矜轶 - Overwhelming and surpassing; describes a painting that is superior and impressive.
  • 抟裂顿斫 - Crushing and cutting; describes a painting that is bold and forceful.
  • 作气满前 - Creating a full presence; describes a painting that is powerful and commanding.
  • 入神家 - Entering the realm of the divine; refers to the highest level of artistic achievement.
  • 名家 - Famous artists; refers to artists who are well-known and respected.
  • 當家 - Master artists; refers to artists who are skilled and proficient.
  • 作家 - Artists who create; refers to artists who produce original works.
  • 匠者家 - Craftsmen; refers to artists who are skilled in their craft but may lack creativity.
  • 吾唯不离乎作家 - I only adhere to being an artist who creates; indicates a commitment to producing original works.
  • 以负此嗛也 - To bear this dissatisfaction; suggests a desire to improve and achieve higher standards.

  • 醉而卧 - Means 'drunk and lay down.' '卧' means 'to lie down.'
  • 殊怏怏 - Means 'very disappointed.' '怏怏' means 'disappointed.'
  • 因辞去，不复通 - Means 'therefore he took his leave and never communicated again.' '辞去' means 'to take leave,' and '不复通' means 'never communicated again.'
  • 未几 - Means 'shortly after.' '未几' means 'in a short time.'
  • 侯生下第 - Means 'Hou Sheng failed the imperial examination.' '下第' means 'to fail the examination.'

  • 本妇睨视数次，忽长叹，潸然泪下 - Describes the wife's reactions, including glancing several times, suddenly sighing, and shedding tears.
  • 偷儿心惊，专心伺之 - Means 'the thieves were startled and watched intently,' showing their reaction to the supernatural event.
  • 妇即自理绢帛，仍有不忍即行之状 - Describes the wife's actions, including handling silk fabrics and showing reluctance to leave.
  • 鬼妇更复再拜祈求 - Means 'the ghostly woman knelt and prayed again,' continuing her supplication.
  • 本妇方行自缢 - Means 'the wife finally hanged herself,' describing the tragic outcome.
  • 偷儿急甚，大声疾呼 - Means 'the thieves were very anxious and shouted loudly,' showing their urgency.
  • 其夫鼾呼若不闻 - Means 'his husband snored as if he didn't hear,' indicating the husband's obliviousness.
  • 偷儿无法以救 - Means 'the thieves had no way to save her,' describing their helplessness.
  • 适檐下有竹竿，取从窗棂中撺击鬼妇 - Means 'just then, there was a bamboo pole under the eaves, which they grabbed and thrust through the window to strike the ghostly woman,' describing the thieves' desperate action.
  • 其夫方觉 - Means 'his husband finally woke up,' indicating the husband's awakening.
  • 偷儿呼令急为开门，相助解救 - Means 'the thieves shouted for him to quickly open the door and help rescue her,' showing their attempt to seek assistance.
  • 事后，各道其详 - Means 'afterward, they each recounted the details,' describing the aftermath.
  • 因发床侧之壁视之 - Means 'they then uncovered the wall beside the bed to examine it,' showing the investigation.
  • 其中梁畔实有先年自缢绳头尚存 - Means 'there was indeed a rope used for hanging that had been there for years,' revealing the hidden truth.
  • 虽云朽烂非真 - Means 'although it was said to be decayed and not real,' indicating skepticism.
  • 而其形其迹，则仍宛然 - Means 'but its shape and traces were still clearly visible,' confirming the presence of the rope.
  • 由此以观 - Means 'from this perspective,' leading to a conclusion.
  • 则凡世俗所传 - Means 'then all the stories passed down in the common folk,' referring to popular beliefs.
  • 亦未尽属无根之谈、荒唐之论矣 - Means 'are not entirely baseless or absurd,' suggesting that some elements of these stories might have a basis in reality.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鬼妇 - Refers to a ghostly woman, a common theme in Chinese folklore and ghost stories.
  • 自缢 - Refers to the act of hanging oneself, often associated with suicide in Chinese culture.
  • 偷儿 - Refers to thieves, a common theme in moral tales and cautionary stories.
  • 竹竿 - Refers to a bamboo pole, a common household item in ancient China.
  • 窗棂 - Refers to window lattice, a traditional architectural feature.
  • 床侧之壁 - Refers to the wall beside the bed, a specific location in the house.
  • 自缢绳头 - Refers to the end of a rope used for hanging, often associated with suicide.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 及讯贼“曷不奔窜，而屈首受擒？” - Direct question to the captives, asking why they did not flee instead of surrendering.
  • 佥曰 - Term indicating that all the captives spoke in unison or agreement.
  • 方将遁，恍惚有赤面长髯大将，乘白马自天而下，指挥神兵，八面旋绕不得脱 - Vivid description of a divine or supernatural intervention, where the captives saw a red-faced, bearded general riding a white horse, commanding divine soldiers, and surrounding them from all sides.
  • 余始惊异，旋问我军，所见无异辞 - Sequence describing the speaker's surprise, followed by questioning their own troops, and the confirmation of their observations.
  • 日既晡，振旅归，亟登城谒帝 - Sequence describing the passage of time (it was evening), the gathering of the troops, and the urgent return to the city to report to the emperor.
  • 仰见帝面汗浃如雨，如甫释甲状，益加悚惕，叩首谢 - Description of the speaker's reaction upon seeing the emperor, who was sweating profusely, as if just released from a state of tension, and the speaker's deep respect and gratitude.
  • 自惟凉德，何敢辱帝力？ - Self-deprecating expression, where the speaker reflects on their own lack of virtue and wonders how they could have burdened the emperor.
  • 或者正可胜邪，诚可回天？ - Rhetorical question expressing doubt about whether the victory was due to the emperor's power or if it could have changed the course of events.
  • 今兹平苗斩妖，不请一兵，不伤一民者，真神助，非人力也！ - Statement attributing the success of the campaign to divine assistance rather than human effort.
  • 余何人斯，敢妄据天功哉？ - Self-deprecating expression, where the speaker questions their own worthiness to claim such a divine victory.
  • 爰是新庙貌，肃几宴，远近奔走者日盛 - Sequence describing the construction of a new temple, the solemn setup for rituals, and the increasing number of people coming to pay respects.
  • 邑人士作《平妖传》，及诗歌传奇纪事，谓百年来所未有 - Sequence describing the creation of a literary work and poems, and the recognition of the event as unprecedented.
  • 苗患遂不复作，今又二十余稔矣 - Statement declaring the permanent elimination of the threat from the rebels.
  • 每岁七夕，余必斋肃祀帝，无忘厥功 - Sequence describing the annual ritual of fasting and worshipping the emperor on the seventh day of the seventh lunar month to remember his achievements.
  • 独怪帝乘马故赤色，此独白 - Note of curiosity about the color of the horse the emperor was riding, which was white instead of red.
  • 或疑马援尝伏五溪蛮，得毋伏波将军来耶？ - Speculation that the white horse might be a reference to General Ma Yuan (伏波将军), who once subdued the Five Streams barbarians.
  • 余谓不然，神像既汗浃示灵爽矣 - Speaker's response, stating that they do not believe this and that the divine image's sweating already indicates its spiritual presence.
  • 余非疑乘马者非帝也，疑帝之马何以白也，姑阙疑以俟考 - Clarification that the speaker is not questioning whether the rider is the emperor but is curious about why the emperor's horse is white and leaves this as a point for further investigation.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 拎明初某勋戚家畜一白马，肥且健 - The phrase '拎明初' is likely a typographical error; '明初' refers to the early Ming dynasty.
  • 一夕关帝梦示云：某省寇乱，欲假而马助兵 - The sentence structure is elliptical, with '关帝梦示云' introducing the content of the dream.
  • 旦起视厩中马，僵卧不起，盖摄其神往矣 - The sentence structure is elliptical, with '盖摄其神往矣' providing the explanation for the horse's immobility.
  • 迨奏凯，勋戚益敬服 - The sentence structure is elliptical, with '迨奏凯' marking the time of the campaign's victory.
  • 京师人异之，因建白马庙奉帝 - The sentence structure is elliptical, with '因建白马庙奉帝' providing the consequence of the people's reaction.
  • 自是帝现身显灵，捍倭破贼，辄骑白马以为常 - The sentence structure is elliptical, with '自是帝现身显灵' marking the beginning of the deity's appearances and '辄骑白马以为常' describing the deity's regular practice.
  • 今大司马遂宁张公尝云尔 - The sentence structure is elliptical, with '尝云尔' summarizing General Zhang's statement.
  • 则城步平苗神异，信哉为帝无疑也 - The sentence structure is elliptical, with '则城步平苗神异' providing the specific event and '信哉为帝无疑也' confirming the deity's identity.
  • 特旧传帝驭赤兔马，一日千里，岂一蹶不复振耶？抑久用而瘏，用人间马协力耶？ - The sentence structure is elliptical, with '特旧传帝驭赤兔马' introducing the legend and '岂一蹶不复振耶？抑久用而瘏，用人间马协力耶？' posing two possible explanations.
  • 附识以资传闻之异云 - The sentence structure is elliptical, with '附识以资传闻之异云' summarizing the purpose of the addition.

  • 大抵神物不世出，有主则灵 - Generally, divine objects do not appear in every generation, but they become effective when they have a master.   • 巫水之木，安知非感王公正气，为弹压溪蛮百世不复萌乱之兆耶 - How can we know that the wood from Wushui is not a sign of the righteous spirit of Governor Wang, which will suppress the Ximan people for a hundred generations and prevent further rebellion?   • 江南之木感于梦，则一介不可妄取 - The wood from Jiangnan, which was felt in a dream, should not be taken lightly. '江南之木' (jiāngnán zhī mù) means 'the wood from Jiangnan,' '感于梦' (gǎn yú mèng) means 'felt in a dream,' and '一介不可妄取' (yī jiè bù kě wàng qǔ) means 'should not be taken lightly.'

  • 如周宣王鼎为严嵩祟者 - Like the tripod of King Xuan of Zhou, which became a demon for Yan Song. '如' (rú) means like, '周宣王鼎' (zhōu xuān wáng dǐng) is the tripod of King Xuan of Zhou, and '为严嵩祟者' (wéi yán sōng suì zhě) means 'became a demon for Yan Song.'

  • 焚香诵佛 - Burning incense and reciting the Buddha are common Buddhist practices, indicating a religious or spiritual context.
  • 官人 - A respectful term for a man, often used in classical literature to address a husband or a respected male figure.

  • 闽葛 - Hemp from Fujian Province, known for its quality, suggests a connection to a specific region.

  • 铜峰 - 'Copper Peak' is likely a fictional or symbolic name, possibly representing a new or different location.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不忮不求 - A phrase from the *Book of Songs* (*Shijing*), meaning 'without jealousy and without greed.'

  • 艺完 - '艺' (yì) means 'art' or 'literary work,' and '完' (wán) means 'completed.'
  • 置于白云山土地香炉下 - '置于' (zhì yú) means 'to place,' '白云山' (báiyún shān) means 'White Cloud Mountain,' '土地' (tǔdì) means 'land god' or 'earth deity,' and '香炉下' (xiānglú xià) means 'under the incense burner.'
  • 圈点叠加 - '圈点' (quān diǎn) means 'circles and dots' (used for marking text), and '叠加' (dié jiā) means 'layered' or 'superimposed.'

  • 笔如悬针倒薤 - '笔' (bǐ) means 'pen' or 'brush,' '悬针' (xuán zhēn) means 'hanging needle,' and '倒薤' (dǎo xiè) means 'upside-down leek,' describing the style of the writing.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 千崖雨湿松添老 - Poetic expression where '千崖' (thousands of cliffs) and '雨湿' (rain-soaked) set a scene of natural beauty, '松添老' (pines grow older) suggests the passage of time and the enduring nature of the pines.
  • 一味秋声菊转新 - '一味' (a single flavor) and '秋声' (sounds of autumn) evoke the essence of autumn, while '菊转新' (chrysanthemums turn new) indicates the renewal and freshness of the season.
  • 莫谓山中无甲子 - Proverbial expression meaning 'do not say that there is no record of years in the mountains,' suggesting that even in remote places, time passes and can be measured.
  • 素珠粒粒纪时辰 - '素珠' (white beads) and '纪时辰' (record the hours) suggest a method of keeping time using beads, akin to a prayer rosary.
  • 崚嶒高石寺门横 - '崚嶒' (jagged and towering) and '高石' (high rocks) describe the grandeur of the temple entrance, '寺门横' (temple gate stands) emphasizes the imposing presence of the temple.
  • 面面波光一派清 - '面面' (all around) and '波光' (ripples of water) create a vivid image of the surrounding environment, '一派清' (all is clear) suggests the purity and clarity of the scene.
  • 鳌背凿开罗汉寺 - '鳌背' (tortoise back) and '凿开' (carved out) refer to the temple being built on a rock formation resembling a tortoise's back, '罗汉寺' (Arhat Temple) is the name of the temple.
  • 龙麟幻出梵天城 - '龙麟' (dragon scales) and '幻出' (appear as) suggest a magical transformation, '梵天城' (Brahma City) is a reference to a celestial city.
  • 昔之异人，隐于屠钓 - '昔之异人' (ancient extraordinary people) and '隐于屠钓' (hid among butchers and fishermen) indicate that wise or powerful individuals often lived incognito in humble occupations.
  • 今之异人，隐于乞丐 - '今之异人' (modern extraordinary people) and '隐于乞丐' (hid among beggars) suggests that such individuals still exist but may be found in even more humble roles.
  • 李丐诗不止于此，今姑择其尤者录之 - '李丐诗' (poetry by a beggar) and '不止于此' (not limited to this) indicate that the beggar's poetry is extensive, '今姑择其尤者录之' (now I will select the best ones to record) shows the editor's selection process.

  • 全字省三画为士，当有姓带土者 - '省三画' means 'reduce three strokes,' resulting in the character '士' (scholar), implying a person with a surname containing the radical '土' (earth).

  • 省两画纵横为木，当有名属木者 - '省两画纵横' means 'reduce two strokes horizontally and vertically,' resulting in the character '木' (wood), implying a person with a name related to 'wood.'
  • 以所省之文全归之，当有名全者 - '所省之文' means 'the reduced text,' and '全归之' means 'all return to it,' implying a person with the name '全.'
  • 已而拜莆田、贵池、元城、涿州四相，一如其言 - '已而' means 'later,' and '一如其言' means 'just as he said,' indicating that the prediction came true.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其事颇涉怪 - The matter involves quite a bit of the strange; the word '颇' (pō) adds emphasis, indicating that the strangeness is significant.
  • 为儒者所不道 - Is not discussed by scholars; '所不道' indicates a negative action performed by a group.
  • 岁时致祀 - To offer sacrifices seasonally; '岁时' (suìshí) refers to the annual cycle of seasons.
  • 以示国家怀柔百神之意 - To show the intention of the state to be gentle and accommodating to all deities; '怀柔' (huáiróu) means 'to be gentle and accommodating.'
  • 不必实有其人也 - It is not necessary that there actually be such a person; '不必' (bùbì) indicates that something is not required or necessary.
  • 袍服酬对 - To respond in formal attire; '袍服' (páofú) refers to formal or ceremonial clothing.
  • 与人世达官无异 - Is no different from high-ranking officials in the world; '无异' (wúyì) means 'no different.'
  • 又世外仙人 - As well as immortals from outside the world; '世外' (shìwài) means 'outside the world' or 'beyond the mundane.'
  • 惝恍难信 - Difficult to believe; '惝恍' (chǎnghuǎng) describes a state of uncertainty or confusion.
  • 而枢亲见之于城市中 - And I personally saw it in the city; '枢' (shū) refers to the narrator, and '亲见' (qīnjiàn) means 'to see personally.'
  • 城隍目为真人 - The city god regards him as a true immortal; '城隍' (chéng huáng) refers to the city god, and '真人' (zhēn rén) means 'true immortal' or 'real person.'
  • 必非妖魅可托 - It must not be a demon or ghost that can be relied upon; '妖魅' (yāo mèi) refers to demons or ghosts.
  • 至天师爵秩相承 - The title of Celestial Master has been passed down through generations; '爵秩' (jué zhì) refers to titles and ranks.
  • 数千年来 - For thousands of years; '数千' (shù qiān) means 'thousands.'
  • 白洙泗外 - Besides the sages of the Zhou and Qi regions; '洙泗' (zhū sì) refers to the rivers near the hometown of Confucius.
  • 鲜与比盛 - Rarely comparable in grandeur; '鲜' (xiǎn) means 'rarely,' and '与比盛' (yǔ bǐ shèng) means 'comparable in grandeur.'
  • 今以其移覆中丞公书观之 - Now, looking at his letter to the Governor; '移覆' (yí fù) means 'to send a letter' or 'to write a letter.'
  • 则封号亦不为倖致也 - Then the title was not obtained by chance; '倖致' (xìng zhì) means 'obtained by chance.'
  • 然非中丞公重士恤灾 - But it was not until the Governor valued scholars and cared for disasters; '重士' (zhòng shì) means 'to value scholars,' and '恤灾' (xù zāi) means 'to care for disasters.'
  • 委曲救拔 - To rescue with great effort; '委曲' (wěi qū) means 'with great effort' or 'indirectly.'
  • 亦安能使天师建醮遣神若是哉 - How could he make the Celestial Master perform rituals and send deities like this? '建醮' (jiàn jiào) means 'to perform rituals,' and '遣神' (qiǎn shén) means 'to send deities.'
  • 枢语余云 - The narrator told me; '语余' (yǔ yú) means 'told me.'
  • 方哑时 - When I was mute; '方' (fāng) means 'when,' and '哑' (yǎ) means 'mute.'
  • 友人母病 - My friend's mother was ill; '友人' (yǒu rén) means 'friend.'
  • 意中若有所叩 - As if something was being asked of me; '意中' (yì zhōng) means 'in my mind,' and '若有所叩' (ruò yǒu suǒ kòu) means 'as if something was being asked.'
  • 忽信笔书云 - Suddenly wrote with confidence; '信笔' (xìn bǐ) means 'with confidence.'
  • 丁丑丁丑 - On the day Ding-Chou; '丁丑' (dīng chǒu) refers to a specific day in the Chinese zodiac calendar.
  • 二人相守 - Two people holding on to each other; '相守' (xiāng shǒu) means 'to hold on to each other.'
  • 玉兔东升 - The Jade Rabbit rises in the east; '玉兔' (yù tù) is a poetic reference to the moon.
  • 大家撒手 - Everyone lets go; '撒手' (sā shǒu) means 'to let go.'
  • 至今不知其所以然也 - To this day, I do not know why; '所以然' (suǒ yǐ rán) means 'why' or 'the reason.'
  • 尤足诧异云 - Especially surprising, it is said; '尤足' (yóu zú) means 'especially,' and '诧异' (chà yì) means 'surprising.'
  • 张山来曰 - Zhang Shanlai said; '曰' (yuē) means 'said.'
  • 天师有如此法力 - The Celestial Master has such power; '法力' (fǎ lì) means 'power' or 'magical ability.'
  • 其世袭也固宜 - It is indeed fitting that it is hereditary; '固宜' (gù yí) means 'indeed fitting.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 城隍 - Refers to the city god, a deity in Chinese folk religion and Taoism.
  • 山川、社稷坛 - Refers to the deities of mountains and rivers, and the altars of the state, which are important in traditional Chinese religion.
  • 儒者 - Refers to Confucian scholars or adherents of Confucianism.
  • 中丞公 - Refers to a governor or high-ranking official.
  • 天师 - Refers to the Celestial Master, a title given to leaders of Taoist orders.

  • 遣神 - Refers to sending deities or spirits.
  • 张山来 - A historical figure, likely a scholar or official.
  • 丁丑 - A specific day in the Chinese zodiac calendar, corresponding to a particular year, month, and day.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '其事颇涉怪，为儒者所不道' is elliptical and must be read as a single statement about the unusual nature of the story and its exclusion from scholarly discourse.
  • The sentence '岁时致祀，以示国家怀柔百神之意，不必实有其人也' explains the purpose of seasonal sacrifices and the symbolic nature of the deities.
  • The sentence '袍服酬对，与人世达官无异' compares the formal attire and responses of the city god to those of high-ranking officials.
  • The sentence '又世外仙人，惝恍难信，而枢亲见之于城市中' introduces the concept of immortals and the narrator's personal experience.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 因祷土神前 - Praying before the earth god; ritual act of invoking the earth deity.
  • 愿剖心活吾父 - Wishing to cut out my heart to save my father; hyperbolic expression of extreme filial piety.
  • 呼饮 - Calling for drink; implies a moment of urgency or distress.
  • 不甚楚 - Did not hurt much; understated description of pain.
  • 创即敛好 - The wound healed immediately; hyperbolic statement of rapid healing.
  • 宜秘 - It should be kept secret; formal instruction to maintain secrecy.
  • 闻伤心 - Heard the news and was heartbroken; compound expression of grief.
  • 错愕 - Startled; expression of surprise and disbelief.

  • 喜出望外 - Overjoyed; expression of extreme happiness and relief.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 土神 - The earth god; a deity in Chinese folk religion and mythology associated with agriculture and the land.
  • 孝子 - A filial son; a person who shows extreme devotion to their parents, often performing acts of self-sacrifice.
  • 医 - The doctor; a medical practitioner in ancient China, often with a combination of traditional and mystical knowledge.
  • 药 - Medicine; in this context, may have magical or supernatural properties.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '因祷土神前，愿剖心活吾父' is elliptical and can be read as 'Because I prayed to the earth god, I wished to cut out my heart to save my father.'
  • The phrase '夜半，吾父呼饮时' can be read as 'At midnight, when my father called for drink,' but it could also imply a moment of urgency or distress.
  • The sentence '予引刀刺胸，出心，割若许，纳饮中以进' is a detailed and graphic description of the act of self-sacrifice.
  • The phrase '不意吾父果霍然也' can be read as 'Unexpectedly, my father was completely cured,' but it could also imply a miraculous recovery.
  • The sentence '当刺胸时，不甚楚，割毕，创即敛好，如未刺时' describes the unusual nature of the event, with the wound healing immediately after being cut.
  • The phrase '宜秘，若勿语' is a clear instruction to keep the event secret, but it could also imply a sense of shame or fear.
  • The sentence '其妻哀，且闻伤心，恐死，亟白之医' is a straightforward narrative, but it could also imply a sense of desperation and urgency.
  • The sentence '医错愕曰：“吁！是顾安所得药？”' is a clear expression of surprise and disbelief, but it could also imply a sense of skepticism.
  • The sentence '妻长跽泣请，医不可却，妄出药涂之去，言必死' is a detailed description of the interaction, but it could also imply a sense of resignation and hopelessness.
  • The sentence '药忽迸落，创痕俱失所在矣' is a magical and unexpected outcome, but it could also imply a sense of wonder and amazement.
  • The sentence '妻喜出望外，促孝子诣医报谢' is a clear narrative, but it could also imply a sense of relief and gratitude.
  • The sentence '医复错愕曰：“吁！是顾安所得活？殆有异！”' is a clear expression of surprise and curiosity, but it could also imply a sense of awe and wonder.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 人觚 - Play on words combining '人' (person) and '觚' (a type of wine vessel), possibly referring to a person who is both upright and refined.
  • 督学江南 - Refers to being the supervisor of education in Jiangnan (the southern region of China).
  • 试卷皆亲自批阅 - Means 'all examination papers were personally reviewed,' emphasizing the thoroughness and personal involvement.
  • 鳞摊诸卷于上 - Vivid description meaning 'arranged like fish scales on the table,' indicating a large number of papers spread out.
  • 左右置酒一坛，剑一口 - Means 'a large jar of wine and a sword placed on either side,' setting a scene of both celebration and vigilance.
  • 手操不律，一目数行 - Means 'holding a ruler in hand and reading several lines at a glance,' describing the efficient and precise manner of reviewing.
  • 每得佳篇，辄浮大白 - Means 'every time he found a good essay, he would take a large drink,' indicating a celebration of good work.
  • 遇荒缪者，则舞剑一回 - Means 'when encountering absurd or poorly written essays, he would dance with the sword once,' symbolizing a way to express frustration or dissatisfaction.
  • 甄拔无遗 - Means 'selecting and promoting without leaving anyone behind,' highlighting the fairness and thoroughness of the selection process.
  • 吾吴冯梦龙，亦其门下士也 - Means 'My friend Feng Menglong from Wu is also one of his disciples,' introducing a key figure.
  • 浮薄子弟，靡然倾动 - Means 'the superficial and frivolous young people were greatly influenced,' indicating the impact of Feng Menglong's works.
  • 至有覆家破产者 - Means 'some even lost their homes and fortunes,' showing the extreme consequences of their actions.
  • 其父兄群起讦之，事不可解 - Means 'his parents and brothers rose up to accuse him, and the matter could not be resolved,' indicating a serious family dispute.
  • 适熊公在告 - Means 'just as General Xiong was on leave,' providing a context for the timing of events.
  • 泛舟西江，求解于熊 - Means 'he sailed to the West River to seek a resolution from General Xiong,' indicating the effort to resolve the dispute.
  • 冯跼蹐不敢置对 - Means 'Feng was so nervous that he dared not respond,' showing his anxiety.
  • 唯唯引咎 - Means 'he humbly admitted his fault,' indicating a submissive attitude.
  • 因致千里求援之意 - Means 'he expressed his intention to seek help over a long distance,' indicating the need for assistance.
  • 此易事，毋足虑也 - Means 'This is an easy matter, there is no need to worry,' indicating confidence in the solution.
  • 我且饭子，徐为子筹之 - Means 'I will first treat you to a meal, and then I will think about it,' showing hospitality and consideration.
  • 须臾，供枯鱼焦腐二簋，粟饭一盂 - Means 'in a short while, they served two bowls of dried fish and burnt food, and one bowl of millet rice,' describing a simple meal.
  • 冯下箸有难色 - Means 'Feng hesitated to eat the food,' indicating his discomfort.
  • 晨选嘉肴，夕谋精粲 - Means 'in the morning, they select fine dishes, and in the evening, they plan elaborate meals,' indicating the usual lavish lifestyle.
  • 似此草具，当非所以待子 - Means 'such a simple meal is not the way to treat you,' indicating the contrast between the usual treatment and the current situation.

  • 西山 - Western Hills, a range of mountains near Beijing.
  • 东岳 - Mount Tai, one of the Five Great Mountains of China and a significant cultural and religious site.
  • 泰山 - Another name for Mount Tai.
  • 泰安州 - Taian, a city in Shandong Province, historically known as the capital of Mount Tai.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Read '东至德州，入山东境' as a single unit indicating movement and location.
  • Read '昔搏徒卖浆，毛公、薛公，以及东方生、管公明，皆奇士' as a single unit listing historical figures.
  • Read '平原君庙内有颜鲁公碑' as a single unit indicating the presence of a stele.
  • Read '惜匆匆过，未见也' as a single unit expressing regret.
  • Read '东南至齐河' as a single unit indicating direction and destination.
  • Read '自涿州背西山而南，七日走九百里' as a single unit describing a journey.
  • Read '极目平畴' as a single unit describing the landscape.
  • Read '齐河水清，抱县城如碧玉环' as a single unit describing the river.
  • Read '石桥跨之' as a single unit describing the bridge.
  • Read '两岸桃柳，新绿嫣红，临水映发，为徘徊桥上者移时' as a single unit describing the scenery.
  • Read '南四十里曰开山，遂入山' as a single unit indicating distance and direction.
  • Read '途中矫首欲望东岳，而适微雨' as a single unit describing the journey.
  • Read '云山历乱，时于云外见高峰，以为是矣' as a single unit describing the landscape.
  • Read '曾不数里，又有高者' as a single unit describing the landscape.
  • Read '午后见一峰甚高，怪石突起，烟岚拥护，谓必是矣' as a single unit describing a mountain peak.
  • Read '已而川势东开，山形北较，远而望之，更有高者' as a single unit describing the landscape.
  • Read '盖余从泰山之北来，午前见背，午后见臂，至泰安州始当其面，而又值云封，故终日望而未之见也' as a single unit describing the journey.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次早欲上 - Literal meaning 'early next morning wanted to ascend,' indicating the intention to climb the mountain early the following day.
  • 土人云 - Means 'the locals say,' indicating a local perspective or advice.
  • 不可，山顶有娘娘庙，领官票而后得入 - Literal meaning 'cannot, there is a Lady Temple at the summit, must obtain an official ticket to enter,' where '娘娘庙' refers to a temple dedicated to a female deity.
  • 票银人二钱，曰口税 - Literal meaning 'the ticket costs two cash per person, called 'mouth tax,' where '口税' is a tax levied per person.
  • 东岳自有神 - Literal meaning 'the Eastern Peak (Mount Tai) has its own deity,' referring to the mountain's spiritual significance.
  • 所谓“娘娘庙”者，始于何代？功德何等？ - Literal meaning 'as for the 'Lady Temple,' from which dynasty did it originate? What are its merits?' questioning the history and significance of the temple.
  • 愚民引夫妇奔走求福 - Literal meaning 'simple folk lead husbands and wives to run around seeking blessings,' describing the behavior of the common people.
  • 为民上者既不能禁，又因以为利 - Literal meaning 'those in power cannot stop it, yet they profit from it,' criticizing the authorities' passive exploitation.
  • 不得已，亦领票，得票欲上 - Literal meaning 'reluctantly, they also obtain a ticket, and with the ticket, they wish to ascend,' showing the process of obtaining permission.
  • 人又云 - Means 'others say,' introducing another piece of advice or information.
  • 山之高四十里，穷日乃至其颠 - Literal meaning 'the mountain is forty li high, and one can only reach its peak by the end of the day,' describing the mountain's height and the difficulty of the climb.
  • 兹向午已迟，且天阴 - Literal meaning 'it is already late in the morning, and the weather is overcast,' indicating the time and weather conditions.
  • 下晴上犹阴，下阴上必雨 - Literal meaning 'if it is sunny below but still overcast above, it will rain if it is overcast below,' predicting weather changes.
  • 雨湿风冷，请以异日 - Literal meaning 'it will be wet and cold with rain, so please wait for another day,' advising against the climb due to unfavorable conditions.
  • 因而观城中之庙 - Literal meaning 'therefore, they observe the temple in the city,' describing a change in plans.
  • 庙去城之南门二百步许，而以北城为后垣 - Literal meaning 'the temple is about two hundred steps from the south gate of the city, with the northern city wall as its rear wall,' providing details about the temple's location.
  • 一城之中，庙居大半焉 - Literal meaning 'in the entire city, temples occupy about half of the space,' emphasizing the prevalence of temples.
  • 阶墀多古柏，云汉武东封时所植 - Literal meaning 'the steps and platforms are lined with ancient cypress trees, said to have been planted during the Eastern Expedition of Emperor Wu of Han,' describing the temple's surroundings.
  • 阶墀有碑，其文曰 - Literal meaning 'there is a stele on the steps and platforms, inscribed with the following text,' introducing a historical inscription.
  • 磅礴东海之西，中国之东，参穹秀灵，生同天地，形势巍然 - Literal meaning 'majestic and vast, it stands west of the East Sea and east of China, with a splendid and spiritual presence, born of heaven and earth, with a grand and imposing form,' describing the mountain's grandeur.
  • 古者帝王登之以观沧海，察地利，以安民生 - Literal meaning 'ancient emperors ascended it to view the vast sea, examine the land's advantages, and ensure the well-being of the people,' explaining the mountain's historical significance.
  • 祝曰：『泰山于敬则致，于礼则宜。自唐加神之号封，历代相沿至今 - Literal meaning 'the prayer says: 'Mount Tai is worthy of reverence and proper in ritual. Since the Tang dynasty, it has been consecrated with divine titles, and this tradition has continued through the ages,' describing the mountain's religious importance.
  • 曩者元君失驭，海内鼎沸，生民涂炭 - Literal meaning 'in the past, when the Yuan ruler lost control, the country was in turmoil, and the people suffered greatly,' referring to a historical period of chaos.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 娘娘庙 - A temple dedicated to a female deity, often associated with local folklore and religious practices.
  • 东岳 - Mount Tai, one of the Five Great Mountains of China, known for its spiritual and cultural significance.
  • 汉武东封 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Han's eastern expedition, a significant historical event.
  • 泰山于敬则致，于礼则宜 - A traditional saying emphasizing the importance of Mount Tai in Chinese culture and religion.
  • 元君失驭 - Refers to a period of political instability and chaos, likely alluding to the Yuan dynasty's decline.

Proper Nouns

  • 东岳 - Mount Tai, one of the Five Great Mountains of China.
  • 汉武 - Emperor Wu of Han, a significant historical figure.
  • 元君 - Refers to a ruler, likely from the Yuan dynasty.
  • 泰山 - Mount Tai, a significant mountain in Chinese culture and religion.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 予起布衣 - Humble self-reference meaning 'I rose from commoner status.'
  • 承上天后土之命 - Literally 'received the mandate of Heaven and Earth,' a euphemistic way of saying 'was chosen by divine will.'
  • 百神阴佑 - Means 'the hundred spirits secretly protected,' referring to unseen divine assistance.
  • 削平暴乱 - Means 'to eliminate violent disturbances' or 'to pacify rebellions.'
  • 正位称职 - Means 'to assume the proper position and fulfill one's duties.'
  • 奉天地，享鬼神 - Means 'to serve Heaven and Earth, and to honor the spirits.'
  • 以依时统一人民 - Means 'to unify the people according to the times.'
  • 法当式古 - Means 'the law should follow ancient precedents.'
  • 特修祀仪 - Means 'especially to revise the rites of sacrifice.'
  • 畏不敢效 - Means 'fearful and unwilling to imitate.'
  • 盖神与穹昊同始 - Means 'the deity and the vast sky have the same origin.'
  • 灵镇一方 - Means 'the spirit governs one region.'
  • 其来不知岁月几何 - Means 'its origin is unknown, and the length of time is indeterminate.'
  • 神之所以灵，人莫能测 - Means 'the reason for the deity's power is beyond human comprehension.'
  • 其职受命于上天后土 - Means 'its duty is ordained by Heaven and Earth.'
  • 为人君者何敢与焉 - Means 'how dare a ruler interfere?'
  • 惧不敢加号 - Means 'fearful and unwilling to add a title.'
  • 特以『东岳之神』名其名 - Means 'especially naming it 'the God of Mount Tai.'
  • 依时祭神 - Means 'to worship the deity according to the times.'
  • 唯神鉴之 - Means 'may the deity witness this.'
  • 洪武三年六月二十日 - Refers to a specific date during the Hongwu era of the Ming dynasty.
  • 可谓辞严义正矣 - Means 'this can be said to be solemn and just.'
  • 庙中望山顶如屏风 - Means 'from the temple, the mountain peak looks like a screen.'
  • 中挂白练 - Means 'a white silk banner hangs in the middle.'
  • 南天门也 - Means 'it is the Southern Heavenly Gate.'
  • 起二更 - Means 'to start at the second watch of the night.'
  • 奋力急趋 - Means 'to rush forward with great effort.'
  • 鸡鸣至其颠 - Means 'to reach the top at the crow of the rooster.'
  • 可观沧海日出也 - Means 'one can observe the sunrise over the vast sea.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 承上天后土之命 - Refers to the Mandate of Heaven, a key concept in Chinese political philosophy.
  • 百神阴佑 - Reflects the belief in divine protection and the role of spirits in governance.
  • 削平暴乱 - Refers to the pacification of rebellions, a common theme in imperial narratives.
  • 正位称职 - Refers to the proper assumption of power and the fulfillment of duties.
  • 奉天地，享鬼神 - Highlights the emperor's role in serving Heaven and Earth and honoring the spirits.
  • 以依时统一人民 - Emphasizes the importance of unifying the people according to the times.
  • 法当式古 - Suggests that laws should follow ancient precedents, reflecting a conservative approach to governance.
  • 特修祀仪 - Indicates the revision of sacrificial rites, a significant act in imperial ceremonies.
  • 东岳之神 - Refers to the God of Mount Tai, a major deity in Chinese religion.
  • 洪武三年六月二十日 - Refers to a specific date during the Hongwu era of the Ming dynasty, indicating the historical context.
  • 庙中望山顶如屏风 - Describes the view from the temple, highlighting the natural beauty and religious significance of the location.
  • 南天门也 - Refers to the Southern Heavenly Gate, a symbolic and religious site.
  • 起二更，奋力急趋，鸡鸣至其颠 - Describes a specific journey, emphasizing the effort and timing involved.
  • 可观沧海日出也 - Suggests the spiritual and aesthetic value of observing the sunrise over the sea.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 如约起 - Formal expression meaning 'as agreed, he rose.' The phrase '如约' indicates that the action was done according to an agreement or expectation.
  • 遥见火光明灭 - Means 'distantly seeing the firelight flickering,' describing a visual scene of firelight that is intermittently visible.
  • 高与星乱 - Means 'as high as the stars, mingling with them,' suggesting that the firelight appears to blend with the stars in the sky.
  • 宿止道旁 - Means 'staying overnight by the roadside,' describing the location where the people are resting.
  • 然炬以丐钱 - Means 'holding torches to beg for money,' describing the action of the people who are using torches to solicit alms.
  • 教养失而民鲜耻 - Means 'when education and moral cultivation are lost, the people have little sense of shame,' expressing a moral critique.
  • 可慨已 - Means 'this is indeed something to lament,' indicating a sense of sorrow or regret.
  • 山足曰红门 - Means 'at the foot of the mountain is called Red Gate,' naming a specific location.
  • 红门以后，路皆石阶 - Means 'after Red Gate, the path is all stone steps,' describing the terrain.
  • 时闻阶旁潺潺有水声 - Means 'occasionally hearing the sound of water trickling beside the steps,' describing an auditory experience.
  • 四更至回马岭 - Means 'at the fourth watch (around 3 AM) they reached Horseback Mountain,' indicating a specific time and location.
  • 阶级愈峻，如行壁上 - Means 'the steps became steeper, as if walking on a wall,' describing the difficulty of the ascent.
  • 鸡鸣至玉皇庙 - Means 'at the crow of the rooster (dawn) they reached the Jade Emperor Temple,' indicating another specific time and location.
  • 导者笑曰：“甫半耳！” - Means 'the guide laughed and said, 'We've only reached halfway!'' indicating a response to the travelers' progress.
  • 因少憇 - Means 'so they took a short rest,' describing an action taken due to fatigue.
  • 黎明，缘涧水，度石桥 - Means 'at dawn, they followed the stream, crossing a stone bridge,' describing the early morning journey.
  • 见两峰对立，中有瀑布 - Means 'they saw two peaks standing opposite each other, with a waterfall in between,' describing a scenic view.
  • 时宿雨初晴，朝光澄彻 - Means 'at the time, after a night of rain, the morning light was clear and bright,' describing the weather conditions.
  • 山岚护石，松翠浮空 - Means 'the mist around the mountain protects the rocks, and the green pines float in the air,' describing the natural scenery.
  • 瀑流飞响，清心韵耳 - Means 'the waterfall's sound flies and echoes, refreshing the mind and pleasing the ears,' describing the auditory and emotional impact of the waterfall.
  • 磴道从西峰上，有碑，题曰“五大夫松” - Means 'the path of stone steps ascended from the western peak, with a stele inscribed 'Five Gentlemen Pines,''' naming a specific landmark.
  • 碑下仰望，见两峰之顶，高插云霄 - Means 'looking up from below the stele, they saw the tops of the two peaks reaching high into the clouds,' describing a visual experience.
  • 心中窃拟谓此山颠也 - Means 'in their hearts, they secretly thought this was the summit of the mountain,' expressing a mistaken assumption.
  • 攀登久之，回首遐眺 - Means 'after climbing for a long time, they looked back and gazed far into the distance,' describing an action and its result.
  • 见松山顶在我足下 - Means 'they saw the summit of the pine-covered mountain beneath their feet,' describing a change in perspective.
  • 昨所见诸峰，在松山下 - Means 'the peaks they saw yesterday were now below the pine-covered mountain,' describing a shift in relative position.
  • 齐鲁数千里之山，又在诸峰之下 - Means 'the mountains of Qi and Lu, spanning thousands of miles, were now below those peaks,' describing a broader geographical context.
  • 盖已飘飘凌云矣，不意峰回路转，更见高峰 - Means 'it seemed as if they were already soaring among the clouds, but unexpectedly, as the path turned, they saw even higher peaks,' describing a surprising discovery.

  • 五大夫松 - Refers to the Five Gentlemen Pines, a specific grove of pines named after a historical or legendary figure.
  • 齐鲁 - Refers to the regions of Qi and Lu, ancient states in China, symbolizing a vast and historically significant area.
  • 宿雨初晴 - Refers to the weather condition of a night of rain followed by a clear morning, a common poetic motif in Chinese literature.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天門之峰 - Refers to the peak of Tianmen Mountain, a famous landmark in Mount Tai (Tai Shan), known for its natural beauty and cultural significance.
  • 無點土，亦無寸草 - A poetic description of the peak's barrenness, emphasizing its stark, rocky nature.
  • 石脈長而廉隅四出 - Describes the long, jagged ridges of rock extending outward, creating a rugged landscape.
  • 骈植叠累 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'arranged in layers like plants,' used to describe the stacked and layered appearance of the rocks.
  • 皱若莲菊 - A simile comparing the texture of the rocks to the wrinkles of lotus and chrysanthemum leaves, highlighting their intricate patterns.
  • 磴道直上十里 - Refers to a steep, straight path leading up the mountain for a distance of ten li (about 5 kilometers).
  • 仰之弥高 - A phrase meaning 'the higher one looks, the higher it seems,' often used to express the grandeur and majesty of something.
  • 中贯铁索 - Describes the iron chains that run through the rock columns, providing support for climbers.
  • 授索而登，抱柱而息 - Describes the method of climbing using the iron chains and resting against the rock columns.
  • 比磴道尽，反无所见 - A paradoxical statement meaning that once the path ends, one cannot see anything, suggesting a moment of revelation or realization.
  • 迂回攀跻 - Describes the winding and difficult ascent.
  • 甘冽 - Describes the water as sweet and refreshing.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 举头天外，俯视寰中 - A vivid description of looking up to the sky and down to the world, emphasizing the vastness and perspective.
  • 浩浩茫茫，四无涯际 - Describes the vast and boundless expanse of the world, with '浩浩茫茫' meaning 'vast and boundless' and '四无涯际' meaning 'no boundaries in any direction.'
  • 东见青营，负山阻海 - Describes seeing the eastern camp, which is backed by mountains and blocked by the sea, indicating a strategic location.
  • 北顾塞垣，横亘千里 - Refers to looking north and seeing the long wall stretching for thousands of miles, emphasizing its length and grandeur.
  • 河朔诸州，星罗棋布 - Describes the states of the Hebei region as scattered like stars, indicating their numerous and dispersed nature.
  • 循太行而西，中州之沃衍，咸阳之阻隘，皆可指数 - Describes following the Taihang Mountains westward, where the fertile lands of the Central Plains and the strategic pass of Xianyang can be counted on one's fingers, emphasizing the ease of enumeration.
  • 黄河由华阴走兖、徐，湾环若衣带 - Describes the Yellow River flowing through Huayin, Yanzhou, and Xu Prefectures, winding like a belt, emphasizing its meandering path.
  • 嵩山二室，如二卷石 - Describes the two peaks of Mount Song as resembling two rolled stones, emphasizing their distinctive shape.
  • 淮阳之间，一望平芜 - Describes the flat grasslands between Huaiyang, emphasizing the vast and open landscape.
  • 登泰山而小天下 - A well-known idiom meaning 'to look down on the world from Mount Tai,' indicating a sense of superiority or perspective.
  • 果不诬也 - Means 'indeed it is not false,' confirming the truth of the previous statement.
  • 峰颠有殿，庭中石崛起 - Describes a temple on the mountain peak with a stone rising in the courtyard, emphasizing the temple's presence and the stone's prominence.
  • 意古者金泥玉检文皆封于此 - Suggests that ancient texts sealed with gold and jade were stored here, indicating a place of historical significance.
  • 门前石表，始皇所建，高二丈余而无字 - Describes a stone tablet in front of the temple, built by Qin Shi Huang, standing over two zhang high but without any inscriptions, emphasizing its historical importance and the lack of text.
  • 日观在东，月观在西，高皆与秦观等 - Describes the Sun and Moon Observatories on the east and west sides, both as high as the Qin Observatory, emphasizing their equal height.
  • 古迹名胜，不可遍睹 - Means 'ancient sites and scenic spots cannot all be seen,' indicating the vastness and complexity of the area.
  • 薄暮遂下，至松山而少憩 - Describes descending in the evening and resting at Song Mountain, emphasizing the journey and the rest.
  • 回思三观，如在天上 - Means 'looking back at the three observatories, they seem to be in heaven,' emphasizing their height and the perspective from below.
  • 又下见朝阳洞，石穴幽邃 - Describes seeing the Dongyang Cave below, with a deep and mysterious stone cave, emphasizing the cave's depth and mystery.
  • 又下见水帘洞，流水蔽岩 - Describes seeing the Water Curtain Cave below, with water flowing over the rocks, emphasizing the cave's natural beauty.
  • 下至山麓，见一巨人，与之并立，翘足伸手，而不能摹其顶 - Describes reaching the foot of the mountain and seeing a giant, standing next to whom, one cannot reach the top with outstretched feet and hands, emphasizing the giant's immense size.
  • 古者长狄在齐、鲁之间，岂其遗种与 - Suggests that the giant might be a descendant of the ancient Longdi people who lived between Qi and Lu, indicating a possible historical connection.

  • 環 - Means 'to encircle' or 'to surround,' describing the position of the water around the city.
  • 蹲 - Means 'to crouch' or 'to sit,' used metaphorically to describe the position of the mountain.
  • 相去 - Means 'the distance between,' used to measure the distance between two places.
  • 俯視 - Means 'to look down upon' or 'to gaze down at,' indicating a change in perspective.
  • 數武 - Means 'a few steps,' used to describe a short distance.
  • 其高可想矣 - Means 'one can imagine how high it is,' indicating the height of the mountain.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 入曲阜之北门 - Entering the northern gate of Qufu.
  • 路东有复圣庙 - To the east of the road is the Temple of the Second Sage.
  • 庙前有陋巷 - In front of the temple is a humble lane.
  • 巷南折而西 - The lane turns south and then west.
  • 孔庙之东华门也 - It leads to the East Hua Gate of the Confucius Temple.
  • 庙制如内廷宫殿 - The temple's architecture is similar to that of the inner court palaces.
  • 柱以石为之 - The columns are made of stone.
  • 蛟龙盘旋 - Dragons are carved in swirling patterns.
  • 至圣与诸贤皆塑像 - The images of the Supreme Sage and the other sages are all in statues.
  • 石刻至圣像有三 - There are three stone carvings of the Supreme Sage.
  • 车服礼器，藏于衍圣公家 - The chariots, clothing, and ritual objects are stored in the home of the Duke Yansheng.
  • 圣公入觐，不可得观 - When the Duke Yansheng visits the court, these items cannot be seen.
  • 殿南有亭，颜曰“杏坛” - To the south of the hall is a pavilion named 'Scholar's Platform'.
  • 古杏数株，时值三月，杏花正开 - There are several ancient apricot trees, and in March, the apricot blossoms are in full bloom.
  • 坛南有先师手植桧 - To the south of the platform is a cypress tree planted by the Master himself.
  • 高三丈而无枝 - It stands three zhang high without branches.
  • 文皆左纽 - Its grain is all twisted to the left.
  • 子贡之楷，虽不腐而色枯 - Zigan's楷tree, though not decayed, has a withered color.
  • 此则生气勃发焉 - This one, however, is full of vitality.
  • 大门内外丰碑无数 - Inside and outside the main gate, there are countless large stone tablets.
  • 南有高楼曰奎文阁 - To the south is a tall building called the Writings Pavilion.
  • 阁南门下，汉、魏之碑十余，皆额尖而有圆孔 - Below the south gate of the pavilion, there are more than ten steles from the Han and Wei dynasties, all with pointed tops and round holes.
  • 门外有水，上作五桥 - Outside the gate is a body of water with five bridges crossing it.
  • 桥南有门，门外有栅 - To the south of the bridge is a gate, and outside the gate is a fence.
  • 自殿庭至栅内，苍松古柏，虬龙盘屈，不可名状 - From the hall to the fence, there are ancient cypress and pine trees, their twisted branches like dragons, too numerous to describe.
  • 泰安汉柏，又不足道矣 - The ancient pines of Tai'an, moreover, are not even worth mentioning.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吾於是奮然興也 - Personal expression meaning 'I thus rise with determination.'
  • 天所獨生以教後世者也 - Formal expression meaning 'one uniquely born by Heaven to teach future generations.'
  • 考其生平 - Means 'to examine his entire life.'
  • 三歲喪父，七歲喪母 - Literal descriptions of early-life losses.
  • 中年出妻，晚年喪子 - Describes further personal tragedies in middle and late life.
  • 哀死而傷離 - Means 'to mourn death and feel sorrow over separation.'
  • 寧獨異於人哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'How could he be any different from others in this regard?'
  • 人觀“志學”一章 - Refers to the chapter 'Aspiration to Learning' in the *Analects*.
  • 七十年内，日进月益 - Means 'over seventy years, daily progress and monthly improvement.'
  • 不以遇之穷而少辍其功 - Means 'not to diminish his efforts because of difficult circumstances.'
  • 蓋其自待厚，而所见有大焉者矣 - Means 'it is because he holds himself to high standards and has great aspirations.'
  • 余乃戚戚欲以身殉，何其陋也 - Means 'I am so distressed that I want to sacrifice myself, how narrow-minded is this!'
  • 《诗》有之曰 - Means 'The *Book of Poetry* says.'
  • 高山仰止，景行行止 - Famous saying from the *Book of Poetry* meaning 'Look up to the high mountains; follow the bright paths.'
  • 虽不能至，然心向往之 - Means 'Even if one cannot reach them, one's heart still yearns for them.'
