Idiomatic Expressions

  • 曲阜东南有九龙山 - Geographical description indicating the location of the Nine Dragons Mountain to the southeast of Qufu.
  • 其南曰马鞍山 - Describes the location of Maanshan to the south of the Nine Dragons Mountain.
  • 两山之间，松楸茂密者，孟林也 - Indicates that between the two mountains, where there is dense growth of cypress and pine trees, is the Meng Forest.
  • 林南为邹县 - States that south of the Meng Forest is Zou County.
  • 县南有孟庙 - Indicates that south of Zou County is the Meng Temple.
  • 庙左有宣献夫人祠 - Describes the location of the Xuanxian Lady's Shrine to the left of the Meng Temple.
  • 夫人者，孟母也 - Identifies the lady as Meng Mu, the mother of Mencius.
  • 滕县在邹南，地平旷，可以行井田 - Describes Teng County, which is to the south of Zou, with flat and open land suitable for implementing the well-field system.
  • 滕南有峄山，始皇刻石其上 - Indicates that to the south of Teng is Yishan, where Emperor Qin Shi Huang inscribed stones.
  • 峄东有陶河，过陶河至邳州 - Describes the location of Tao River to the east of Yishan, and the journey through the Tao River to Pizhou.
  • 下邳乃子房击秦后潜匿之所 - States that Xiapu is the place where Zhang Liang, after defeating the Qin, went into hiding.
  • 又项籍者，下相人也 - Identifies Xiang Yu as a native of Xiang, which is in Pizhou.
  • 下相在邳州 - Confirms that Xiang is located in Pizhou.
  • 昔曹操决水灌吕布于下邳 - Describes how Cao Cao once flooded the city of Xiapu to defeat Lü Bu.
  • 今其地在山，不可灌 - States that now the area is mountainous and cannot be flooded.
  • 予尝徘徊其地，求下邳、下相之故城，及圯桥进履之所，而土人皆无知者 - Describes the author's search for the ancient cities of Xiapu and Xiang, as well as the bridge where Zhang Liang met the old man, but finding no locals who know about them.
  • 邳南落马湖，黄河所溢也 - Indicates that to the south of Pizhou is Luomahu Lake, which is part of the Yellow River's overflow.
  • 湖南曰宿迁，宋人迁宿于此 - States that to the south of the lake is Suqian, where the Song people migrated.
  • 又南曰桃源，乃黄河之北岸也 - Describes that further south is Taoyuan, which is on the northern bank of the Yellow River.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 地多湖，四望皆水 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the land is filled with lakes, and all around one sees water.'
  • 江北暮春，似河北之盛夏 - Comparative phrase meaning 'the late spring in Jiangbei resembles the hot summer in Hebei.'
  • 草长成茵，麦秀成浪 - Poetic description of grass growing thickly like a mat and wheat rippling like waves.
  • 花剩余红，树凝浓绿 - Vivid description of flowers still retaining their red color and trees with dense, deep green foliage.
  • 扬州自古繁华地，当南北水陆之冲 - Statement meaning 'Yangzhou has been a prosperous place since ancient times, located at the intersection of north-south water and land routes.'
  • 舟车辐辏，士女游冶 - Phrase describing the bustling traffic of boats and carriages, with men and women enjoying leisurely outings.
  • 兼以盐商聚处，僭拟无度，流俗相效，竞以奢靡 - Description of salt merchants gathering, behaving without restraint, and influencing the common people to indulge in extravagance.
  • 城内无可观，隋宫、迷楼、二十四桥之胜迹，今皆不存 - Statement meaning 'there is nothing remarkable to see inside the city; the famous sites of the Sui palace, the Miliu, and the Twenty-Four Bridges no longer exist.'
  • 琼花观内，止余故址 - Phrase meaning 'inside the Qionghua Temple, only the ruins remain.'
  • 城北有天宁寺，谢东山之别业也 - Statement indicating that to the north of the city is the Tianning Temple, which was the private residence of Xie An.
  • 余尝寓杏园之僧舍，竹树蓊郁，池台清幽 - Personal anecdote meaning 'I once stayed in a monk's lodging in Xingyuan, where the bamboo and trees were lush, and the ponds and terraces were serene.'
  • 想见王谢风流 - Phrase meaning 'one can imagine the elegance of the Wang and Xie families.'
  • 杏园东曰虹桥，园亭罗列水次 - Description indicating that to the east of Xingyuan is the Hongqiao Bridge, with gardens and pavilions arranged along the water.
  • 游人棹酒船于其中 - Phrase meaning 'tourists paddle their wine boats among them.'
  • 虹桥之北，则蜀岗也 - Statement meaning 'north of the Hongqiao Bridge is Shugang.'
  • 欧阳文忠公建平山堂于其上 - Phrase meaning 'Ouyang Wenzhong Gong built the Pingshan Hall on it.'
  • 堂右有大明寺井，昔张又新作《煎茶水记》，谓扬子江中泠泉第一，惠山石泉第二，虎丘石井第三，丹阳寺井第四，扬州大明寺井第五 - Historical reference indicating that to the right of the hall is the well of the Daming Temple, which was mentioned by Zhang Youxin in his *Record of Tea Water* as the fifth-best spring.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至于 - A transitional phrase meaning 'as for' or 'reaching up to,' used to introduce the geographical and historical descriptions.
  • 昔 - An adverb meaning 'in the past' or 'formerly,' used to indicate historical context.
  • 梦以手捧日者再 - A vivid and idiomatic expression meaning 'dreamed of holding the sun with his hands twice,' symbolizing great ambition or aspiration.
  • 颜曰 - A phrase meaning 'named it' or 'called it,' indicating the naming of a place or object.
  • 遂渡江 - A phrase meaning 'then crossed the river,' indicating a sequence of actions.
  • 扬子江阔而清 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'the Yangtze River is broad and clear,' emphasizing the river's characteristics.
  • 含虚混碧 - A poetic phrase meaning 'contains the void and mixes with the blue,' describing the river's appearance.
  • 上下澄鲜 - A phrase meaning 'both above and below are clear and fresh,' describing the river's transparency.
  • 金、焦在中 - A phrase meaning 'Mountains Jin and Jiao are in the middle,' indicating the presence of specific landmarks.
  • 如踞镜面 - A metaphorical phrase meaning 'like sitting on a mirror surface,' describing the smooth and reflective nature of the river.
  • 四面皆楼阁 - A phrase meaning 'surrounded by pavilions and towers,' describing the architecture around the mountain.
  • 靓妆刻节 - A phrase meaning 'elaborately decorated and carved,' describing the ornate appearance of the buildings.
  • 远望焦山 - A phrase meaning 'looking far at Mount Jiao,' indicating a distant view.
  • 林木青苍 - A phrase meaning 'trees are green and dark,' describing the forested landscape.
  • 土人云 - A phrase meaning 'the locals say,' indicating a local tradition or belief.
  • 焦山山里寺 - A phrase meaning 'Mount Jiao has a temple inside,' describing the location of a temple.
  • 金山寺里山 - A phrase meaning 'Mount Jin has a temple inside,' describing the location of a temple.
  • 惜余未上 - A phrase meaning 'I regret not having climbed it,' expressing the author's regret.
  • 于焦止见山 - A phrase meaning 'at Mount Jiao, I only saw the mountain,' indicating what the author saw.
  • 于金止见寺 - A phrase meaning 'at Mount Jin, I only saw the temple,' indicating what the author saw.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 过江 - Means 'to cross the river,' referring to crossing the Yangtze River.
  • 四面阻山 - Means 'blocked by mountains on all sides,' indicating a strategic geographical location.
  • 形格势禁 - Means 'geographically constrained and strategically limited,' emphasizing the defensive nature of the area.
  • 以临天堑 - Means 'to face the natural barrier,' referring to the Yangtze River as a natural defensive line.
  • 实南北必争之地 - Means 'a place that must be contested by the north and south,' highlighting its strategic importance.
  • 始都此 - Means 'first established as a capital here,' indicating the historical significance of the location.
  • 筑城名曰“铁瓮” - Means 'built a city named 'Iron Pot',' referring to the ancient name of Zhenjiang.
  • 府城其遗也 - Means 'the city of the prefecture is its legacy,' indicating that the current city is the remnant of the ancient city.
  • 南至于丹阳 - Means 'south to Danyang,' indicating the direction of travel.
  • 闻有练湖而未见 - Means 'heard of Lake Lian but not seen it,' indicating the author's awareness of the lake but lack of personal experience.
  • 东南至常州 - Means 'east-south to Changzhou,' indicating the direction of travel.
  • 古延陵地 - Means 'ancient Yancheng land,' referring to the historical name of Changzhou.
  • 吴季子之所居 - Means 'the place where the Duke of Wu Ji Zi lived,' referring to the historical figure.
  • 俗在三吴为淳朴 - Means 'the customs in the three Wu regions are simple and honest,' indicating the cultural characteristics of the region.
  • 其南峰曰惠山 - Means 'its southern peak is called Huishan,' indicating the name of a mountain peak.
  • 惠山之东曰锡山 - Means 'east of Huishan is called Xishan,' indicating the name of another mountain.
  • 峰峦皆秀丽 - Means 'all peaks are beautiful,' describing the scenery.
  • 登惠山，领石泉，清冽而甘且厚 - Means 'climbing Huishan, leading to the stone spring, clear and cool, sweet and rich,' describing the experience of visiting the mountain.
  • 下视无锡，群山拱峙，众水环流 - Means 'looking down at Wuxi, surrounded by mountains, with many waters flowing around,' describing the view from the mountain.
  • 名酒嘉鱼菱藕之薮，乐土也 - Means 'a place famous for fine wine, delicious fish, lotus roots, and water chestnuts, a land of happiness,' describing the local products and lifestyle.
  • 昔泰伯择居于此 - Means 'formerly Tai Bo chose to live here,' referring to the historical figure.
  • 惠山之南曰夫椒 - Means 'south of Huishan is called Fuchao,' indicating the name of a mountain.
  • 夫差败越之所也 - Means 'the place where Fu Chai defeated Yue,' referring to a historical battle.
  • 夫椒之南曰阳山 - Means 'south of Fuchao is called Yangshan,' indicating the name of another mountain.
  • 越败夫差之地也 - Means 'the place where Yue defeated Fu Chai,' referring to another historical battle.
  • 阳山以南，群峰列峙，巍然而葱郁者，灵岩、穹隆、支硎、玄墓、上方诸山也 - Means 'south of Yangshan, a series of peaks stand tall and lush, including Lingyan, Qionglong, Zhixing, Xuanmu, and Shangfang Mountains,' describing the landscape.
  • 灵岩之东，树林阴翳，有秀出于树中者，虎丘也 - Means 'east of Lingyan, a forest is shaded, with a beautiful peak standing out among the trees, called Tiger Hill,' describing the scenery.
  • 虎丘南六七里，苏州城也 - Means 'six to seven miles south of Tiger Hill is Suzhou City,' indicating the location of Suzhou.
  • 姑苏控三江、跨五湖而通海 - Means 'Gusu controls the three rivers, spans the five lakes, and connects to the sea,' describing the geographical features of Suzhou.
  • 阊门内外，居货山积，行人水流，列肆招牌，灿若云锦 - Means 'inside and outside the Gate of Chuan, goods pile up like mountains, people flow like water, and shop signs shine like brocade,' describing the bustling market.
  • 语其繁华，都门不逮 - Means 'speaking of its prosperity, even the capital cannot match it,' describing the wealth and prosperity of Suzhou.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 然俗浮靡 - Phrase meaning 'yet the customs are frivolous and luxurious,' describing a societal trend toward superficiality and excess.
  • 人夸诈 - Means 'people boast and deceive,' highlighting a social atmosphere of pretension and dishonesty.
  • 百工士庶，殚智竭力 - Literally 'craftsmen and commoners exhaust their wisdom and strength,' referring to the people who put great effort into creating elaborate and ornamental works.
  • 奇技淫巧 - Term meaning 'extraordinary skills and frivolous craftsmanship,' often used to criticize excessive and impractical artistry.
  • 作无益以害有益者与 - Rhetorical question meaning 'is it not making something harmful to benefit from it?' This phrase criticizes the wastefulness of focusing on trivial pursuits at the expense of more important matters.
  • 虎丘小而奇 - Description of the Tiger Hill, saying it is small but unique or remarkable.
  • 外望一土阜 - Means 'from the outside, it looks like a small hill,' describing the appearance of the hill from a distance.
  • 中有洞壑 - Indicates that inside, there are caves and ravines, emphasizing the contrast between the exterior and interior of the hill.
  • 路旁岩下，有泉曰憨泉 - Describes a spring located under a rock beside the path, named 'Huan Spring' (憨泉).
  • 泉侧有石，中裂若劈 - Refers to a stone near the spring that is split in the middle, resembling a cleavage or split.
  • 试剑石 - A name for the split stone, suggesting it was used to test swords.
  • 曲折而上，一大磐石，平铺数百步 - Describes a large flat rock that extends for several steps, requiring a winding path to reach.
  • 千人坐 - A name for the large flat rock, implying it could accommodate many people.
  • 四围奇峰，峭拔若削 - Describes the surrounding peaks as steep and sharp, as if they were cut by a knife.
  • 北辟一壑，中有清池 - Indicates that to the north, there is a ravine containing a clear pool.
  • 剑池 - The name of the pool, suggesting it was associated with swords.
  • 剑池之西，又辟一壑，窈窕幽奇，而亦有池 - Describes another ravine to the west of the Sword Pool, which is deep, secluded, and mysterious, also containing a pool.
  • 虎丘石井 - The name of the pool in the western ravine, suggesting it was a stone well.
  • 剑池之东有亭 - Indicates that there is a pavilion to the east of the Sword Pool.
  • 可中亭 - A name for the pavilion, suggesting it is centrally located or suitable for a central position.
